calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-05-2026 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Oct-23-2025 Continued To Jul-27-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-09-2025 Set For Trial On Jan-26-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-27-2025 Continued To Apr-23-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-21-2025 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS VELVET CANNABIS, AND FARID HARRISON's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants Firefly Health Corp. and Farid Harrison's demurrer to Plaintiff Bianca Viscusi's first amended complaint (FAC) is sustained without leave to amend.

Defendants Firefly and Harrison demur to Plaintiff's intentional infliction of emotional distress (IIED) claim in the FAC. This court sustained, with leave to amend, Defendants' demurrer to the original IIED claim on grounds Plaintiff failed to allege extreme and outrageous conduct, and failed to allege severe emotional distress. (See 8/8/24 Order in ROA.) Defendants demur to this IIED claim, once again contending Plaintiff fails to state a claim. (CCP 430.10(e).)

The FAC suffers from the same deficiencies as the original complaint, namely failure to allege extreme and outrageous conduct. Defendants represent that the only new paragraphs in the FAC are paragraphs 2, 3, 11-37, and Plaintiffs do not disagree. None of these revised or added paragraphs contain new material allegations of Defendants' purported extreme and outrageous conduct.

Order signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS VELVET CANNABIS, AND FARID HARRISON'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Off calendar. The Shafer declaration fails to show that the parties met and conferred "in person, by telephone, or by video conference" in compliance with CCP 430.41. The parties are ordered to comply with the code. The response to the FAC is now due by November 26, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-10-2024 Continued To Nov-13-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT FIREFLY HEALTH CORP, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION DOING BUSINESS AS VELVET CANNABIS AND FARID HARRISON's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part.

First, whether Firefly Health Corporation is a proper party is a factual dispute that can be addressed by discovery. Demurrer overruled.

Second, the complaint inadequately pleads intentional infliction of emotional distress. As plaintiff concedes, 1) to be "outrageous," the pled conduct must be "so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in civilized society - atrocious and utterly intolerable" and (2) plaintiff's "suffering" must be "substantial" or "enduring." (Opp. 9:23-25, 11:13.) The complaint's allegations do not reach that level, and plaintiff largely relies on opinions not binding in our courts. (Id. at 10:13-22.) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)
